name: Neon DB leads dashboard
description:
Dashboard modifications for the IRUS AI Outreach Pipeline:
1. Add a new "Infos" section/tab in the live dashboard showing all scraped lead info (from leads data)
2. Migrate all data storage from CSV files to a Neon PostgreSQL database
3. Store all leads, email drafts, and action logs in the database
4. The connection string is: postgresql://neondb_owner:npg_N70sdZWreHfA@ep-red-meadow-apdboe3l-pooler.c-7.us-east-1.aws.neon.tech/neondb?sslmode=require&channel_binding=require
5. Update the frontend dashboard to display leads info in a new tab alongside "Review & Send" and "Activity Log"
6. All backend modules currently using CSV files should use the database instead
7. Add new API endpoints to serve leads data to the frontend

goal.answer: Migrate the IRUS AI Outreach Pipeline from CSV-based storage to Neon PostgreSQL, add a new "Infos" tab on the live dashboard that displays all scraped leads, and ensure all backend modules read from and write to the database.

criteria.answer:
Neon DB tables exist: leads, email_drafts, action_logs
Existing CSV data (leads, drafts, logs) is migrated to Neon on first startup
Backend modules (lead_finder, lead_enricher, email_drafts, logger) use psycopg2 instead of CSV I/O
GET /leads endpoint returns all scraped leads with optional category/search filters
Dashboard has a third "Infos" tab listing all leads in a responsive table/cards view
Launching a campaign writes leads + drafts to Neon, dashboard reflects it in real time
Existing tabs (Review & Send, Activity Log) and features (edit, send single/all) continue working

constraints.answer: Use the provided Neon connection string, stored in .env (never hardcoded in committed files), Use psycopg2-binary for synchronous DB access to match existing code style, Clear existing drafted/edited rows between campaigns (mirrors current clear_drafts behavior); preserve sent/failed rows as history, Keep .env in .gitignore, Handle DB connection errors gracefully (log, return 500), Table creation uses IF NOT EXISTS to survive restarts, Use a single db.py module for all DB operations to avoid scattered connection logic

phases.answer: P1: Database Infrastructure, P2: Backend Data Layer, P3: API Layer + New Endpoints, P4: Frontend Dashboard, P5: Integration Verification

assumptions.answer: Neon DB is accessible from current environment (outbound TCP 5432); psycopg2-binary installs without system dependencies; Current CSV files contain <200 rows - migration is trivial and fast; Existing CSV column names map 1:1 to DB columns; FastAPI runs in main thread with BackgroundTasks, so a single connection per operation is sufficient; Browser cache from old dev server may require hard refresh

phases.name: P1: Database Infrastructure
phases.goal: Install dependency, create db.py with connection + schema + migration, store DATABASE_URL in .env.

steps.description: Create backend/db.py with get_conn(), init_tables(), and migrate_from_csv()

verification.command: python3 -c "from backend.db import get_conn, init_tables; conn=get_conn(); cur=conn.cursor(); cur.execute(\"SELECT tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public'\"); print([r[0] for r in cur.fetchall()])"

result.stdout: cd backend && source ../venv/bin/activate && python3 -c "from db import get_conn, init_tables; conn=get_conn(); cur=conn.cursor(); cur.execute(\"SELECT tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public'\"); print([r[0] for r in cur.fetchall()])"

steps.description: Store DATABASE_URL in .env and run migration

verification.command: python3 -c "from backend.db import get_conn; conn=get_conn(); cur=conn.cursor(); cur.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM leads'); print('leads', cur.fetchone()[0]); cur.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM email_drafts'); print('drafts', cur.fetchone()[0]); cur.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM action_logs'); print('logs', cur.fetchone()[0])"

result.stdout: cd backend && source ../venv/bin/activate && python3 -c "from db import get_conn; conn=get_conn(); cur=conn.cursor(); cur.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM leads'); print('leads', cur.fetchone()[0]); cur.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM email_drafts'); print('drafts', cur.fetchone()[0]); cur.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM action_logs'); print('logs', cur.fetchone()[0])"

phases.summary: Phase 1 complete: psycopg2-binary installed, backend/db.py created with get_conn(), init_tables(), migrate_from_csv(), and full CRUD helpers. DATABASE_URL stored in .env. Migration successfully imported 17 leads, 8 drafts, and 29 logs into Neon PostgreSQL.

phases.name: P2: Backend Data Layer
phases.goal: Replace all CSV reads/writes in four core modules with equivalent DB operations.

verification.command: python3 -c "from backend.lead_finder import find_leads; print('lead_finder loads')"

result.stdout: python3 -c "from backend.lead_finder import find_leads; print('lead_finder loads')"

steps.description: Rewrite lead_enricher.py to read leads from DB, enrich, UPDATE back to DB

verification.command: python3 -c "from backend.lead_enricher import enrich_leads; print('lead_enricher loads')"

result.stdout: python3 -c "from backend.lead_enricher import enrich_leads; print('lead_enricher loads')"

steps.description: Rewrite email_drafts.py CSV CRUD → DB CRUD; keep sent/failed on clear

verification.command: python3 -c "from backend.email_drafts import get_all_drafts, save_draft, update_draft, clear_drafts; print('email_drafts loads')"

result.stdout: python3 -c "from backend.email_drafts import get_all_drafts, save_draft, update_draft, clear_drafts; print('email_drafts loads')"

steps.description: Rewrite logger.py CSV read/write → DB read/write

verification.command: python3 -c "from backend.logger import write_log, already_contacted, print_summary; print('logger loads')"

result.stdout: python3 -c "from backend.logger import write_log, already_contacted, print_summary; print('logger loads')"

phases.summary: All four backend modules migrated from CSV to DB: lead_finder uses insert_leads, lead_enricher uses get_raw_leads/update_lead, email_drafts wraps db.py CRUD helpers, and logger re-exports db.py functions. All modules verified to import and function correctly against real Neon DB data.

phases.name: P3: API Layer + New Endpoints
phases.goal: Update FastAPI endpoints to use DB, add GET /leads.

steps.description: Update /logs to read from action_logs table instead of log.csv
Add GET /leads endpoint with category and search query params
Verify /drafts, /drafts/pending, PUT /drafts/{email}, POST send endpoints still work

phases.name: P4: Frontend Dashboard
phases.goal: Add Infos tab, fetch leads, render table with filters.

steps.description: Update activeTab state type to include 'infos', add leads state, fetch /leads in polling effect
Render leads in a responsive table/card grid with Name, Org, Email, Phone, Role, Category, City, Source columns
Add category filter buttons (All / Defence / Medical) for the Infos tab

phases.name: P5: Integration Verification
phases.goal: Launch a dry-run campaign, confirm DB writes and dashboard reflection.

steps.description: Trigger POST /launch with a small test campaign

verification.command: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3050/launch -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"domain":"Medical","locations":["Noida"],"selected_products":["Advanced Cancer Detection Vehicle"],"dry_run":true}' | python3 -m json.tool | grep -q 'started'

steps.description: Poll /leads and /drafts/pending to confirm DB writes
Confirm frontend renders without console fetch errors

goal: Migrate the IRUS AI Outreach Pipeline from CSV-based storage to Neon PostgreSQL, add a new "Infos" tab on the live dashboard that displays all scraped leads, and ensure all backend modules read from and write to the database.
successCriteria: Neon DB tables exist: leads, email_drafts, action_logs
Existing CSV data (leads, drafts, logs) is migrated to Neon on first startup
Backend modules (lead_finder, lead_enricher, email_drafts, logger) use psycopg2 instead of CSV I/O
GET /leads endpoint returns all scraped leads with optional category/search filters
Dashboard has a third "Infos" tab listing all leads in a responsive table/cards view
Launching a campaign writes leads + drafts to Neon, dashboard reflects it in real time
Existing tabs (Review & Send, Activity Log) and features (edit, send single/all) continue working

Use psycopg2-binary for synchronous DB access to match existing code style
Clear existing drafted/edited rows between campaigns (mirrors current clear_drafts behavior); preserve sent/failed rows as history
